description_long: This object is a dark, elongated stone with a pointed, leaf-like shape and a rough, chipped surface texture. The stone appears to be made of a dense, fine-grained material, possibly obsidian or flint, with visible flake scars from intentional shaping. Its color is predominantly black with subtle grayish highlights and some lighter speckling on one side. The surface is matte and irregular, with no visible polish or gloss, and the edges are sharp and angular. There are no visible inclusions, carvings, or artificial markings except for a small area of white painted numbers on one side. The overall form is symmetrical along its length, tapering to points at both ends, and it is relatively thin when viewed from the side.
description: A dark, pointed, leaf-shaped stone with a rough, chipped surface and sharp edges, predominantly black with some grayish highlights and a small area of white markings.
description_view_0: The stone is seen from the side, showing its elongated, pointed shape and rough, chipped surface with visible white markings.
description_view_1: A narrow edge-on view reveals the thinness of the stone and its symmetrical tapering to points at both ends.
description_view_2: The opposite side displays the broad, flat surface with more visible flake scars and subtle lighter speckling.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the stone's thin profile and sharp edges.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular flake scars and chipping patterns from shaping, with no regular or artificial decorative patterning.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with some minor surface wear but no significant erosion or lichen growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smoother, man-made surfaces but could blend with other natural stones or gravel in landscaping.
